rulings.Case Title: MARK EMMETT WILSON VS. MIGUEL ALEJANDRO RESCALVO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Deft Rescalvo Responses To Judicial Form Interoggs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 21, 2013, Line 2, PLAINTIFF MARK WILSON's Motion To Compel Defendant Rescalvo Responses To Judicial Form Interrogatories. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff MARK EMMETT WILSON's Motion to Compel Defendant MIGUEL ALEJANDRO RESCALVO's Responses to Form Interrogatories is GRANTED, in part. On or before December 6, 2013, Defendant MIGUEL ALEJANDRO RESCALVO shall provide verifications to the Responses to Form Interrogatories, Set Two, served September 17, 2013. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NONY V. CONCEPCION VS. ZHIYANG CHENG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 21, 2013, Line 17: Defendant ZHIYANG CHENG'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT The motion to strike is granted without leave to amend as to the prayer for punitive damages and the traffic collision report that is attached as an exhibit to the Complaint. Plaintiff does not plead sufficient facts to support the prayer for punitive damages in this simple car accident case. The traffic collision report should not be attached to the complaint to allege a cause of action for negligence and inclusion of the report violates Vehicle Code § 20013. The Court denies the motion to strike as to the remaining items because the moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1322(a) and highlight in the notice of motion precisely what items ought to be stricken. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: ABS SEAFOOD, INC., A CORPORATION VS. BLACK JR., MICHAEL EDWARD, INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Application And Application For Right To Attach Order; Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 21, 2013, Line 19: Plaintiff ABS SEAFOOD, INC.'S Application For Right To Attach Order Denied. Plaintiff ABS Seafood, Inc. applies for a writ of attachment against Defendant Michael Edward Black, Jr., individually and dba Sebo and dba Sebo Restaurant. (Application, 2.) Plaintiff has not established the probable validity of its claim. (CCP § 484.090.) The open book account identifies the account holder as "Sebo", and not Mr. Black. (Ackerman Decl., Exhibit A.) Plaintiff has not presented sufficient evidence to demonstrate that Mr. Black does business as Sebo or Sebo Restaurant. Further, the Complaint is not verified as averred in the Application. (Compare Complaint; with Application, 7a, Cook Decl., p. 1:10-11.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM
